name: Acueducto de Abánades — circular desde Osorno la Mayor
region_intro: Hiking around Osorno La Mayor offers a distinct experience on the Castilian plateau, characterized by the flatlands of Tierra de Campos. The region is primarily defined by vast cultivated fields, moorlands, and riverside groves, irrigated by the Valdavia and Boedo rivers, and the prominent Canal de Castilla. This landscape provides opportunities for walks focused on historical waterways and the tranquil beauty of a plains environment. Best hiking trails around Osorno La Mayor The most popular hiking route is Castillo de las Cabañas de Castilla from Osorno La Mayor by the Canal de Castilla, a 11.9 miles (19.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 53 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the historic Canal de Castilla, showcasing its engineering features. Another top favourite among local hikers is Boadilla del Camino — circular along the banks of the Canal de Castilla, an easy 3.2 miles (5.2 km) path. This circular route offers a gentle walk along the banks of the Canal de Castilla. Local hikers also love the Osornillo y Melgar de Fernamental — circular por Sendero de los Hortelanos (PRC-BU 190), a 15.4 miles (24.8 km) trail leading through cultivated fields and plains, often completed in about 6 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around Osorno La Mayor is defined by walks along the Canal de Castilla, through cultivated fields, and alongside riverside groves. The network offers options for easy walks to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Osorno La Mayor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from around 10 reviews. More than 60 hikers have used komoot to explore Osorno La Mayor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Osorno La Mayor?
A: There are 6 hiking trails documented around Osorno La Mayor on komoot. These routes offer a range of experiences, from easy strolls to more challenging long-distance paths across the Castilian plateau.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Osorno La Mayor?
A: The terrain around Osorno La Mayor is characterized by the flatlands of Tierra de Campos. You'll primarily find walks through vast cultivated fields, interspersed with moorlands and tranquil riverside groves along the Valdavia and Boedo rivers, as well as the historic Canal de Castilla. It's a plains environment, so expect gentle gradients rather than mountainous climbs.

Q: Are there hiking options for all skill levels in Osorno La Mayor?
A: Yes, the area offers routes for various skill levels. There is 1 easy route, 2 moderate routes, and 3 more difficult routes, ensuring options for both casual walkers and those seeking a longer challenge.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Osorno La Mayor?
A: While hiking, you can explore the unique plains environment, including the Valdavia and Boedo rivers, and the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;. Points of interest along the canal include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1012824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quadruple Lock of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; and various aqueducts, showcasing historical engineering.

Q: Is Osorno La Mayor a good place for birdwatching during a hike?
A: Yes, the region is an interesting spot for birdwatching. The cultivated fields and riverside groves provide a habitat for various steppe birds like partridges, snipe, and stone-curlews, as well as storks, ducks, nightingales, falcons, and kestrels in the fluvial areas.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are circular routes available. For example, the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067290423/boadilla-del-camino-circular-along-the-banks-of-the-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boadilla del Camino — circular along the banks of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; offers a gentle walk, and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104394625/osornillo-y-melgar-de-fernamental-circular-por-sendero-de-los-hortelanos-prc-bu-190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osornillo y Melgar de Fernamental — circular por Sendero de los Hortelanos (PRC-BU 190)&lt;/a&gt; is a longer option.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Osorno La Mayor?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most public hiking trails in rural areas like Osorno La Mayor. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through cultivated fields or near livestock, and to check for any specific local regulations or signage on individual routes.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Osorno La Mayor suitable for families with children?
A: Many of the trails, particularly those along the Canal de Castilla, are relatively flat and well-suited for families. The easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067290423/boadilla-del-camino-circular-along-the-banks-of-the-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boadilla del Camino — circular along the banks of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; is a good option for a gentle family outing.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Osorno La Mayor?
A: The best time to hike in Osorno La Mayor is typically during spring and autumn. These seasons offer milder temperatures, making walks across the open plains more comfortable. Summer can be very hot, while winter might bring colder conditions, though the flat terrain generally remains accessible.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Osorno La Mayor?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from around 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil beauty of the plains, the historical significance of the Canal de Castilla, and the opportunities for peaceful walks away from crowded areas.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options available?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer excursions, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093014484/castillo-de-las-cabanas-de-castilla-from-osorno-la-mayor-by-the-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de las Cabañas de Castilla from Osorno La Mayor by the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, at 19.2 km, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104394625/osornillo-y-melgar-de-fernamental-circular-por-sendero-de-los-hortelanos-prc-bu-190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osornillo y Melgar de Fernamental — circular por Sendero de los Hortelanos (PRC-BU 190)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 24.8 km, provide substantial walking experiences.
tips:
Mandatory stop: The King's Bridge or Abánes Aqueduct is one of the most spectacular works of the entire Canal.
It allows the crossing over the Valdavia river over five semicircular arches.

waypoints.name: Puente del Rey Aqueduct (Canal de Castilla)
